channel.title: Drupal CMS
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal CMS.

item.title: #3569529: Add an AGENTS.md file
item.description:
• Project: Drupal CMS
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: aecaffb · +268/-0 production · 10 files
  • Discussion: #3569529 · 4 contributors · 12 comments

Summary

Drupal CMS now includes an AGENTS.md file to help AI coding assistants work more effectively with the codebase. This file provides structured guidance on the project's technology stack, coding standards, development commands, and prohibited actions. The documentation helps AI tools avoid common mistakes and follow project conventions when assisting developers with Drupal CMS contributions.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The new AGENTS.md file documents Drupal CMS-specific conventions for AI coding assistants. It covers the monorepo structure with recipes in the recipes directory, the drupal_cms_helper module, and the project_template directory used for end-user installations.

The file provides command references for common development tasks using DDEV and Drush, including recipe application, PHPUnit testing, site installation, and content export with drush content:export. It specifies coding standards like using strict typing, the ?type nullable syntax, #[\Override] attributes, and marking new classes as final with private members.

Several README files in protected directories (cpanel_template, patches, scripts, splitter) now explicitly warn agents not to modify those areas. The prohibited actions section lists destructive operations like running ddev tag or composer update outside the project root, and prevents agents from adding patches or pinning dependencies in Composer files.

The documentation emphasizes defensive programming in drupal_cms_helper, requiring checks like \Drupal::moduleHandler()->moduleExists() before using optional module functionality. It also instructs agents to mark new non-test classes as internal with specific warning text.

Timeline

  • Opened: Jan 26, 2026
  • Committed: Feb 4, 2026 (9 days later)

Collaboration

This issue demonstrates the Drupal CMS team's pragmatic approach to AI tooling. The issue author acknowledged ambivalence about AI while recognizing its practical utility and the need to accommodate developers using these tools. Multiple contributors provided feedback to improve structure and readability, with discussion about whether to manage the file through a separate package like the scaffold system. The team decided keeping AGENTS.md in the repository made more sense for documenting internal workings.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3568955: Update documentation for 2.0 release
item.description:
• Project: Drupal CMS
  • Type: Normal task
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 5a8c498 · +1119/-2269 production · 130 files
  • Discussion: #3568955 · 2 contributors · 13 comments

Summary

Drupal CMS 2.0 brings a major documentation refresh to help site builders and content editors find what they need faster. The documentation has been reorganized with a clearer structure, splitting "Create" content into "Get Started," "Customize," and "Manage" sections. Many outdated tutorials have been removed or rewritten, screenshots updated, and navigation paths corrected to reflect the 2.0 interface. The changes make it easier to discover features like recommended add-ons, accessibility tools, email setup, hosting guidance, and content management workflows.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

This issue consolidated documentation updates for the Drupal CMS 2.0 release across multiple merge requests. The changes reorganized the information architecture by moving files between directories: content from docs/src/create/ was redistributed to docs/src/get-started/, docs/src/customize/, and in some cases removed entirely. Files moved to customize/ include add-ons, modules, email setup, hosting, consent management, and privacy policy documentation. New content in get-started/ covers site settings and theme customization. Several tutorials were deleted, including workflows, site-settings, and detailed navigation guidance, while others were significantly condensed. The contributor removed verbose "Goal," "Prerequisites," "Next steps," and "Wrap-up" sections from most tutorials to streamline the content. Screenshots were updated throughout to reflect the 2.0 administrative interface, and admin paths were corrected (switching from old-style notation to the newer format). The changes also removed references to features not present in 2.0, such as the Events content type, and updated feature descriptions to match current functionality. Binary image files were deleted or replaced as part of the refresh.

Timeline

  • Opened: Jan 22, 2026
  • First commit: Feb 2, 2026 (11 days later)
  • Last commit: Feb 4, 2026 (2 days later)

Collaboration

This documentation refresh was a collaborative effort between pameeela and phenaproxima over two weeks with three commits. The approach of merging updates in stages made reviews more manageable. Pameeela created the initial merge request and noted it was "better than what we have" even before completion, allowing incremental improvement. After the first merge, pameeela continued refining and reviewed every documentation page before submitting a second merge request. Phenaproxima handled merging to the 2.x branch and cherry-picking necessary changes back to the 2.0.x release branch. This iterative process demonstrates how large documentation projects can be broken into reviewable chunks while still achieving comprehensive coverage.

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.

item.title: #3571163: Add a CONTRIBUTING.md to the project template
item.description:
• Project: Drupal CMS
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Diff: 8f92852 · +46/-12 production · 4 files
  • Discussion: #3571163 · 2 contributors · 7 comments

Summary

Drupal CMS now includes a dedicated CONTRIBUTING.md file that guides potential contributors through the setup process. People who wanted to contribute were cloning the wrong repository and getting stuck because there was no clear documentation. The new file explains the monorepo structure, provides step-by-step DDEV installation instructions, and links to the development issue queue and Slack channel. The README.md was also updated to point to this new contributor guide.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The Drupal CMS project template now includes a CONTRIBUTING.md file in the project_template/ directory. This file addresses a recurring problem where contributors would clone the published Composer package repository instead of the development repository.

The new CONTRIBUTING.md explains that Drupal CMS uses a monorepo structure that automatically splits into smaller projects. It specifies DDEV version 1.24.0 or later as a requirement and provides three commands to get started: clone the repository from git@git.drupal.org:project/drupal_cms.git, change into the directory, and run ddev launch.

The file also documents common DDEV commands like ddev drush and ddev composer, and directs contributors to open issues and merge requests in the Drupal CMS development issue queue. It includes links to the code of conduct, the #drupal-cms-development Slack channel, and DDEV documentation.

The README.md was refactored to remove redundant contribution information and now references CONTRIBUTING.md instead. The "Contributing" section was renamed to "Contributing & Support" and streamlined to focus on reporting issues and getting support, with a link to the new contributor guide.

Timeline

  • Opened: Feb 3, 2026
  • Committed: Feb 3, 2026

This summary is AI-generated and may contain errors. Check the original issue on Drupal.org for the full picture. For more digests, see Drupal Digests.
